message:
feat(dns): negative caching for no-record DNS results with configurable TTL

DnsCache now caches negative no-record results alongside positive
lookups via a CachedIpEntry enum (Positive | NegativeNoRecords). This
prevents repeated DNS queries for hosts that consistently return empty
record sets.

New methods:
- put_negative_ip_no_records(host): caches a negative entry with the
  configured negative_ttl from CacheConfig
- get_ip_result(host): returns Option<Result<LookupIp, DnsError>>,
  where a cached negative entry yields Some(Err(DnsError::NoRecords))
  and a cache miss yields None

The existing get_ip(host) method filters out negative entries and
continues to return Option<LookupIp> for backward compatibility.

Internal refactoring: put_ip and put_negative_ip_no_records share a
common put_ip_entry method that handles TTL clamping, eviction, and
capacity management.

Resolver: updated to use negative caching when DNS resolution returns
no records.

Tests added: cache_negative_no_records_hits_and_expires verifies
case-insensitive negative cache hits, correct DnsError::NoRecords
return, and expiration after negative_ttl.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
